title: Bridge Essay: An Exploratory Functional Near-infrared Spectroscopy Study of Prefrontal Cortex

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Form/Meditation** (Phase 7) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Form/Meditation to mechanistic neuroscience through An, Exploratory, Functional, Near-infrared, Spectroscopy.

**An Exploratory Functional Near-infrared Spectroscopy Study of Prefrontal Cortex Connectivity during Body Scan Meditation.** — Clin Psychopharmacol Neurosci. PMID: 41139603. DOI: 10.9758/cpn.25.1277. Body scan meditation is apopular mindfulness practice in which aperson directs their attention toward internal bodily sensations. Although its neural mechanisms have been investigated using functional magnetic resonance imaging, few studies have used functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) to directly measure prefrontal networks during body scan meditation. In this study, symptoms of depression and anxiety were measured in 40 healthy young adults without prior meditation experience. Part
